block-handbooktext-potioncauldron: Acts like a large residue covered pot, the brewing cauldron is a large vessel too big to fit inside a firepit. A stirring spoon is required to brew potions. Not suitable for cooking food.

alchemy:alchemyguide-text: Alchemy allows you to brew potions that enhance your abilities, grant utility effects, and more. There are three tiers of potions, each requiring progressively more advanced equipment. Tier 1 - Edible Herbs: The earliest form of alchemy. Only a mortar & pestle required. Tier 2 - Medium Potions: Liquid potions brewed in a cauldron firepit. Requires copper age, with a barrel, a cauldron and wooden stirring spoon. Tier 3 - Strong Potions: Can you handle my strongest potions? Requires herb racks, glassworking and aqua vitae. Weapon Coating: Apply potions to your weapons and arrows to deliver their effects on hit. Throwable Flasks: Throw filled flasks to splash potion effects onto all creatures in range on impact. The Alchemy mod is highly configurable. I highly reccomend you use the mod ConfigLib if you want to change anything about this mod, the are hundreds of different config values for you to adjust. If you have any suggestions or find any bugs, preferably let me know on the official Vintage Story discord in the alchemy mod thread, or just leave a comment on the mod page.
alchemy:alchemyguide-herbs-text: Edible herbs are the simplest form of alchemy and require no metal tools. Craft a mortar and pestle to get started. There is a wood variant or stone variant that has greater durability. Gather herbs, flowers, poisonous/psychedelic mushrooms, and horsetail from the wild. Grind poisonous/psychedelic mushrooms and horsetail together in the mortar and pestle to create a basic potion base. Then grind your choice of herbs or flowers into a weak potion base. Combine the weak potion base and the basic potion base in a crafting grid to create an edible herb ball. Edible herbs are consumed directly for a weak effect. Edible herbs: Archer, Potent, Purging, Settling, Hunter, Looter, Fighter, Miner, Spiked, Predator, Healing, Scentmask, Speed, Vitality
alchemy:alchemyguide-medium-text: Medium potions are liquid brews cooked in a brewing cauldron. They are more effective than edible herbs and they are stored in flasks. You will need: a brewing cauldron, a wooden stirring spoon attached to the cauldron, a barrel for making potion tea, and flasks to store and drink the finished potions (You can drink from other liquid containers if config is changed, although flasks handle potion drinking better). Cauldron Setup: Place the cauldron in the world, then right-click it with dry grass in hand, followed by four right-clicks with firewood in hand. The cauldron is now ready to use. Step 1 - Make Potion Tea: Seal a barrel with 1 basic potion base and 2 litres of water. After 24 hours it will turn into potion tea. This is a key ingredient for all liquid potions. Step 2 - Brew the Potion: Place 1 weak potion base and 1 litre of potion tea into the cauldron, attach a wooden stirring spoon, then light the fire beneath. The cauldron will cook the mixture into a medium potion. Step 3 - Fill Flasks: Use a flask or bowl to take the finished potion from the cauldron output slot. Utility potions are brewed the same way using their respective bases with 1 litre of potion tea. Medium potions: Archer's Flask, Potent Oil, Purging Brew, Settling Brew, Hunter's Delight, Looter's Delight, Fighter's Flask, Miner's Flask, Spiked Oil, Predator Pheromones, Healing Oil, Scent Mask, Sprinter's Flask, Vitality Flask Utility potions: Recall Flask, Water Breathing Flask, Glow Flask, Nutrition Flask, Stability Flask, Featherfall Flask, Spider Flask, Growth Flask, Shrinking Flask, Reshape Flask, Warmth Flask
alchemy:alchemyguide-strong-text: Strong potions are the most powerful of brews, requiring glassworking and aqua vitae in addition to the equipment from Tier 2. Additional requirements: a glass stirring spoon (required instead of a wooden spoon), a herb rack for drying your herbs, and aqua vitae. There are many wood and metal variants of herb racks available. Step 1 - Dry Your Herbs: Hang fresh herb or flower raw cuttings on a herb rack. They will slowly dry into dried cuttings. Cuttings are unlikely to spoil while on the rack. Step 2 - Cook the Dried Cuttings: Cook the dried cuttings on a firepit or in a clay oven to create cooked cuttings. Step 3 - Make the Strong Potion Base: Combine cooked cuttings in a crafting grid to create a strong potion base. Step 4 - Brew the Potion: Place 1 strong potion base, 0.25 litres of aqua vitae, and 0.75 litres of potion tea into the cauldron. Attach a glass stirring spoon and light the fire. Potion tea is still made the same way as in Tier 2 (basic potion base + 2L water in a barrel, sealed for 24 hours). Strong potions: Archer's Flask, Potent Oil, Purging Brew, Settling Brew, Hunter's Delight, Looter's Delight, Fighter's Flask, Miner's Flask, Spiked Oil, Predator Pheromones, Healing Oil, Scent Mask, Sprinter's Flask, Vitality Flask
alchemy:alchemyguide-coat-text: Weapon coating lets you apply a potion onto a melee weapon or onto arrows so the potion's effect is delivered to whatever you hit. Great for making a healing club for an ally, or poisoning your enemies. What you need: A coating source held in your OFF hand. This can be an edible herb ball (uses one herb) or a potion flask(uses 0.25 L). A coatable item held in your MAIN hand which is any melee weapon or a arrow (both configurable). How to apply a coating: Hold the coating source in your off hand and the weapon or arrows in your main hand, then hold Shift + Right-click and keep holding for a couple of seconds. Quick coating with a barrel: Place a weapon or a stack of arrows into a barrel of potion to coat them instantly. Weapons are coated to max charge and arrow stacks all at once. Melee weapons: Each coat adds one charge, up to a maximum (5 by default). Every melee hit on a living creature spends one charge and applies the potion effect. Projectiles: Projectiles such as arrows are coated individually rather than by charges. A coated projectile delivers its effect to the first creature it hits. By default it's only arrows. Strength: Coating effects are a fraction of the strength of drinking the same potion (90% by default). If enabled, potions that aren't healing or spiked oil can be used but will only work on players. A weapon can only hold one type of potion at a time at one strength, so hit something to clear a coating before switching potions.
alchemy:alchemyguide-throw-text: Throwable flasks are vessels filled with a potion that shatter on impact, splashing their effect onto all living creatures within range. They come in two variants: glass and ceramic. Glass Throwable Flasks: Available in many colours depending on the ore used (clear quartz, olivine, amethyst, rose quartz, smoky quartz, and others). See: Glass Throwable Flask Ceramic Throwable Flasks: Clay-formed using blue, fire, or red clay colour based on cooking method. See: Ceramic Throwable Flask Filling: Fill a throwable flask the same way as any potion flask — tap right-click on a liquid container. The flask holds exactly one dose. Throwing: Hold the filled flask in your main hand and right-click to wind up and release the throw. On impact: The flask shatters and applies the potion effect to all living creatures within the splash radius (2.5m by default, configurable). Effects are applied at 50% strength by default (configurable). Only potions permitted for weapon coating can be applied this way but still can be thrown. If thrown into liquids the flask will slow rapidly dropping as an intact item without shattering and preserving its contents if its slow enough. Doesn't apply potion effects under water.
alchemy:alchemyguide-z-effects-text:
A glossary of every effect a potion that can be applied and what each one does.

Many effects come in three strengths, shown on the flask as weak, medium and strong. A higher strength only increases the size of the effect, not how long it lasts.

Ranged accuracy: steadies your aim with bows and slings.
Ranged damage: increases damage from arrows and sling rocks.
Ranged speed: speeds up drawing and loading ranged weapons.
Melee damage: increases damage with melee weapons.
Walk speed: increases movement speed on foot.
Hunger rate: changes how fast your food bar drains; a higher rate hungers you faster, a lower rate slower.
Healing effectiveness: affects bandages apply time (config can be used to effect potions drink/eat time as well). It for some reason doesn't change your natural regeneration or the amount healed last checked on v1.22.3 of Vintage story.
Max health: raises your maximum health for the duration.
Restore health: heals you over time.
Poison: damages you over time and ignores armour.
Mining speed: breaks stones and ores faster. Doesn't affect dirt.
Ore amount: increases ore from mined ore blocks.
Animal loot: increases drops from harvesting carcasses. Doesn't apply to rust creatures.
Forage amount: increases drops from foraged berries, mushrooms and ground forage.
Wild crop: increases drops from harvesting wild plants.
Gears from metal pile: increases rusty gears recovered from metal piles.
Vessel contents: increases loot from broken storage vessels.
Animal seek range: changes how far creatures notice you; lowering it hides you, raising it draws them toward you.

Utility effects come in only weak strength and last for the duration shown on the flask:
Glow: you emit light.
Water breathing: your oxygen stays full underwater.
Warmth: your body temperature will not fall below normal.
Temporal stability: a one-time boost to your temporal stability.
Nutrition: evens out your nutrition across all food groups. Usually with a cost of overall nutrition loss.
Recall: teleports you to your spawn point.
Reshape: lets you re-open character selection once with the .charsel command.
Growth: increases your size until death or disconnect.
Shrinking: decreases your size until death or disconnect.
Featherfall: reduces fall damage.
Spider: lets you climb any surface.
Flight: lets you fly freely. You cannot mount anything when the effect is active.

Side effects when drank (a cost paid only when applied):
Damage taken / health restored: a brew may hurt or heal you.
Intoxication: a drunkenness effect, shown as a percentage of maximum.
Psychedelic: a hallucinogenic effect, shown as a percentage of maximum.
Saturation: a change to your food bar level.

Other mods may change how these effects behave and this list may fall out of date.

ScentMaskPotionAnimalSeek-Comment: Scent mask's animal seek range value per tick before strength multiplier
SpeedPotionValue-Comment: Sprinter's flask's walk speed value per tick before strength multiplier
VitalityPotionMaxHealth-Comment: Vitality flask's max health value per tick before strength multiplier
GlowPotionStrength-Comment: Glow flask's light strength, never really fully understood how this worked with the amount but I remember it crashed when the number was above 31. From my testing it didn't really make much of a difference when changing the value.
WaterBreathePotionDuration: Water breathing flask's duration in seconds
NutritionPotionRetainedNutrition: Nutrition flask's retained nutrition after equalization of nutrients
NutritionPotionRetainedNutrition-Comment: 0 means you lose all nutrition and 1 means you retain all nutrition and 2 means you double your nutrition
GrowPotionSizeChange-Comment: Growth flask's height change in blocks per use
ShrinkPotionSizeChange-Comment: Shrinking flask's height change in blocks per use (negative value = shrink)
GrowShrinkMinHeight-Comment: The minimum height in blocks a player can be shrunk to
GrowShrinkMaxHeight-Comment: The maximum height in blocks a player can be grown to
potion-already-active: This potion effect is already active
coating-not-allowed: This potion cannot be used for weapon coating
coating-max-charges: This weapon's coating is already at maximum charges
coating-conflict: This weapon is already coated with a different potion
Allow-WeaponCoating-Config: Allow weapon coatings by potion type
WeaponCoatEffectMultiplier-Comment: Strength of weapon/arrow coating effects as a fraction of the drinkable potion (0.5 = half the strength)
WeaponCoatCharges-Comment: The max number of melee hits a weapon coating can hold
WeaponCoatApplyTime-Comment: How long (in seconds) the player must hold shift+right-click to apply a coating to a weapon
PotionDrinkTime-Comment: How long (in seconds) it takes to drink a potion from a flask (liquidcontent source)
PotionEatTime-Comment: How long (in seconds) it takes to consume a solid potion item (item source)
ScalePotionTimeWithHealing: Scale consume time with healing effectiveness!!!
ScalePotionTimeWithHealing-Comment: If enabled, the time to drink or eat a potion is scaled by the player's healing effectiveness stat (lowered by armour), the same way bandage application time is. Higher effectiveness consumes faster, lower consumes slower
PotionConsumeMaxTimeMultiplier-Comment: When healing effectiveness scaling is enabled, this is the slowest consume time as a multiple of the normal drink/eat time (reached at minimum healing effectiveness). Only used when 'Scale consume time with healing effectiveness' is on
PotionConsumeLitres-Comment: How many litres are consumed each time a potion is drunk from a flask (liquidcontent source)
PotionDrinkCheckLitres-Comment: The minimum litres that must be in a flask before a potion can be drunk. Usually set slightly below the drink amount to avoid floating point issues
WeaponCoatConsumeLitres-Comment: How many litres are consumed each time a coating is applied from a flask (liquidcontent source)
WeaponCoatCheckLitres-Comment: The minimum litres that must be in a flask before a coating can be applied. Usually set slightly below the coat amount to avoid floating point issues
CoatableWeaponTags-Comment: Comma-separated VS item tags for weapons that can be coated with potions. "weapon-melee" is the default. This will mean every vanilla weapon and every modded weapon with that tag can be coated. If you want to only allow specific weapons with different tags to be coated. For example if you replace it with "weapon-sword, tool-cleaver" then only swords and the cleaver will be coatable. MAKE SURE TO HAVE VALID TAGS OR IT WON'T WORK!
CoatableProjectilesCodes-Comment: Comma-separated wildcard item codes for projectiles that can be coated with potions. "*arrow*" is the default, which makes every vanilla and modded arrow coatable. Patterns are matched against the full item code (domain:path), so you can use forms like "*arrow*", "game:arrow-*", or "mymod:bolt-*". For example "game:arrow-*, mymod:bolt-*" allows only vanilla arrows and that mod's bolts. Leave empty to disable projectile coating entirely.
OnlyOnePotionAtATime: Only allow one potion active at a time
AllowPotionExclusivity-Comment: When enabled, each potion is assigned an exclusivity group below. Two potions sharing the same group cannot be active at the same time, and a potion set to 'Solo' cannot be combined with any other potion. 'None' leaves a potion unrestricted. This is a softer alternative to 'Only allow one potion active at a time'. Disabled by default.
PotionGroup-Comment: Exclusivity group for this potion. Potions sharing a group cannot be active together. 'Solo' means it cannot be combined with anything. 'None' leaves it unrestricted. Only has an effect when 'Enable potion exclusivity groups' is on.
AllowPotionRefresh-Comment: Allow drinking a potion that is already active to restart its duration. The old effect is replaced by the new drink, so drinking a different strength of the same potion applies the new strength. Potions with health ticks (regen/poison) cannot be refreshed since their healing/damage over time cannot be cancelled early.
SideEffectStrengthMultiplier-Comment: Side effects (Health change, intoxication, psychedelic effect, saturation loss) are multiplied by potion strength when this is enabled. When disabled side effects are not affected by potion strength and apply the same amount regardless of potion strength.
AllowVanillaContainerDrinking: Allow drinking from other liquid containers
AllowVanillaContainerDrinking-Comment: Allow potions to be drunk from vanilla bowls, buckets, and jugs, as well as Expanded Foods glass bottles when that mod (A Culinary Artillery) is installed. I assume its a little buggy to since the potion flasks handle some interactions differently to vanilla containers.
heldhelp-coat-weapon: Hold in off hand to coat weapon or arrows
DrinkingPotionIntoxicationAmount-Comment: How much intoxication is changed per potion consumed (0 = disabled). This isn't scaled by potion strength. This is a side effect. This applies to ALL POTIONS, for individual potion types go to the extra effect sections below this section!
DrinkingPotionPsychedelicAmount-Comment: How much psychedelic effect is changed per potion consumed (0 = disabled). This isn't scaled by potion strength. This is a side effect. This applies to ALL POTIONS, for individual potion types go to the extra effect sections below this section!
DrinkingPotionSaturationLossAmount-Comme: How much saturation is changed per potion consumed (0 = disabled). This isn't scaled by potion strength. This is a side effect. This applies to ALL POTIONS, for individual potion types go to the extra effect sections below this section!
DrinkingPotionDamageAmount-Comment: How much health is changed on the player per potion consumed (0 = disabled).  This isn't scaled by potion strength. This is a side effect. This applies to ALL POTIONS, for individual potion types go to the extra effect sections below this section!
